title: Chessboard Crossing
summary: Treat the intersection like a board where order matters more than force.
memoryHook: First move the piece that has the rule, not the piece that looks strongest.

title: Gate Guard
summary: A stop sign is a gate that only opens after a full pause and a fresh scan.
memoryHook: Stop, look, then pass through the gate.

title: River Confluence
summary: Yielding is like joining a river only when the current leaves room.
memoryHook: You do not force the water; you wait for the flow.

title: Watchtower Scan
summary: Mirrors and blind spots are a lookout tower for hidden movement.
memoryHook: A tower scan sees more than a glance.

title: Torch Pass
summary: A signal is a torch that tells others where you intend to go.
memoryHook: Show the torch before you step.

title: Lantern Walk
summary: Low visibility is a dark path where slower movement keeps the lantern useful.
memoryHook: Move slow enough that the light still helps.

title: Circle Arch
summary: A roundabout is an arch that only opens when the curve gives you a safe gap.
memoryHook: Do not step under the arch before the opening is yours.

title: Shield Wall
summary: Pedestrians and cyclists deserve a protective wall of caution from the driver.
memoryHook: The wall goes up first; movement comes after.

title: Field Lantern
summary: Speed control is the lantern that keeps the whole field legible.
memoryHook: If the field blurs, slow the lantern down.

title: Reclaim Path
summary: A missed answer is a trail you can walk again if you name the trap and restate the rule.
memoryHook: Name it, rule it, retry it.
